Just published a new research note on bear market Web3 marketing budgets.
Here’s your TL;DR ↓
• In Q1 2026, Galaxy said crypto/blockchain VC deployment was about
$4B,
roughly
50% down QoQ.
• That is the bigger shift: budgets are still there, but they are getting far more selective.
• The article argues that 2027 marketing will move from buying attention to proving activation, retention, and trust.
• Referral, affiliate, CPA, and revenue-share deals should get more popular, but only brands with a strong offer will win good KOLs.
• Pure referral campaigns still need a trust layer – awareness, comparisons, community proof, and third-party mentions.
• Classic paid shilling looks weaker; contextual inclusion inside relevant reports, explainers, and workflows should do better.
• AI search adds another filter: brands need validation across Reddit, YouTube, LinkedIn, reviews, and comparison pages.
• My read: the winners will be the teams that build believable distribution, not just reach.
Read the full story here:
Web3 Marketing in 2027: Adapting to bear market budgets

recent_posts.text:
Morpho defies DeFi slowdown with 86% USDC deposit growth –
Link
Despite overall DeFi TVL falling 42% year over year, Morpho's USDC deposits rose 86% to $2.8 billion. The data points to continued demand for yield-bearing stablecoin lending even as the broader market deleverages.
Matters because:
Capital is concentrating in higher-quality lending venues even while broader DeFi activity weakens.

tweet.text:
USDC deposits into @Morpho continue growing despite a broad DeFi slowdown.

While aggregate DeFi TVL is down ~42% over the past 365 days, USDC deposits into Morpho are up ~86%, reaching ~$2.8B.

From Poker Pro To Trading Giant: Inside The Mind of Selini Capital
"Someone always wants to de-risk an asset they hold. They'll give up edge to secure the bag. You're always exchanging risk for expected value. Understand that primitive and you find every trade."
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U9-OCx4UVJk

The Decline of Deviance - Where has all the weirdness gone?
Wealth made life long, safe, and comfortable, so people stopped taking risks, and both bad deviance (crime, drugs, teen pregnancy) and good deviance (weird art, bold science, counterculture) collapsed together since the 1990s
The internet accelerated but didn't cause this, that starving for art became irrational once poverty got rare and shameful, and that since weirdness is now a choice, ordinary people must act as tolerant gatekeepers who let good deviance live
https://www.experimental-history.com/p/the-decline-of-deviance
https://www.experimental-history.com/p/the-decline-of-deviance-2

A Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age
> Demis Hassabis argues AGI could be one of humanity's most transformative technologies.
> “The magnitude of this technology’s impact will be unprecedented, perhaps 10x of the Industrial Revolution at 10x the speed.”
> However, he warns that accelerating frontier AI requires stronger safeguards, international coordination, and responsible deployment to manage cyber, bio, and other emerging risks.
> He proposes a U.S-led Frontier AI Standards Body to evaluate advanced models, set evolving safety benchmarks, audit frontier labs, and establish a global framework for safe AGI development while preserving innovation.
Source:
https://x.com/demishassabis/status/2076957440109625718

Clarity Act enters critical legislative window –
Link
The U.S. Senate's CLARITY Act entered a crucial four-week window that will decide whether it can pass before Congress adjourns in August. President Trump called for Senate passage, citing competition with China on crypto and AI.
Matters because:
The bill could improve regulatory certainty for U.S. digital asset markets if it advances in time.

tweet.text: Critical week for Clarity, which also happens to be the one-year anniversary of GENIUS. A reminder of the incredible amount of hard work that has gone into this bill, but also of the time we’ve already lost. We cannot afford to delay any longer.

U.S.-Iran tensions amplify oil and inflation pressure –
Link
Escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities triggered reciprocal airstrikes and pushed Brent crude futures more than 3% higher to nearly $79 per barrel. Markets are watching for Strait of Hormuz disruptions that could lift energy prices, keep inflation sticky, and weigh on crypto risk assets.
Matters because:
Energy shocks can feed inflation and make Fed easing harder, which is typically negative for crypto.

• The token traded steadily during broader market consolidation.

Stable liquidity and sustained volume can help a small-cap token hold gains even in choppy markets.

recent_posts.text:
data refineries: turning abundant data into trustworthy, actionable intelligence
this is the moat
https://x.com/michellelsun/status/2076705184898785637

tweet.text:
Data volume is no longer the bottleneck for robotics training. 

It’s building dynamic pipelines - like oil refineries in the AI age - that guarantee trustworthy lineage + usable signal for training and deployment.

A single robot spits out terabytes of raw camera, LiDAR & IMU streams daily. 

Training data supply is exploding globally, especially with the surge of data collection startups across India, China, and beyond.

In order for these data to be useful, we need robust processing pipelines that handle : 
- Provenance tracking (where did this frame come from? which embodiment? under what conditions?)
- Cleaning, synchronization, labeling
- Repeatedly & reliably turning it into high-quality multi-modal tokens for training and real-time deployment

The new moat in robotics is the robust, dynamic data infrastructure that turns raw observations into trustworthy, actionable intelligence.

Who’s building the best data refineries?
